description: Legacy notebooks migrated into the interactive-v1 shell.

notebooks.title: Math 01: Algebra — Linear Equations
objectives: Define what a linear equation is and identify its components
Solve single-variable linear equations using inverse operations
Recognise the three solution cases (unique, none, infinitely many)
Translate algebraic solutions into Python code from scratch
Verify solutions computationally and handle pathological edge cases

notebooks.title: Math 02: Calculus — Derivatives & the Power Rule
objectives: Understand the derivative as the slope of a function at a point
Compute derivatives via the difference quotient (limit definition)
Apply the Power Rule to differentiate polynomials efficiently
Implement numerical and symbolic differentiation in Python

notebooks.title: Math 03: Calculus — The Chain Rule
objectives: Differentiate composite functions using the Chain Rule
Decompose nested functions into layers for differentiation
Implement chain-rule differentiation computationally

notebooks.title: Math 04: Calculus — Local Extrema
objectives: Use derivatives to find a function's local peaks (maxima) and valleys (minima)
Identify critical points where the derivative is 0 or undefined
Apply the First Derivative Test to classify critical points
Implement an extrema-finding algorithm computationally

notebooks.title: Math 05: Linear Algebra — Vectors
objectives: Represent points and displacements in N-dimensional space
Perform vector addition and scalar multiplication
Compute the dot product and understand its geometric meaning
Compute the cross product (for 3D vectors)
Implement vector math computationally from scratch

notebooks.title: Math 06: Linear Algebra — Matrices
objectives: Define matrices and their dimensions
Multiply a matrix by a vector to perform a linear transformation
Multiply two matrices together
Understand the geometric intuition behind matrices
Implement matrix multiplication computationally from scratch

notebooks.title: Math 07: Calculus — Differential Equations
objectives: Solve separable differential equations analytically
Visualize differential equations using Slope Fields
Implement Euler's Method to approximate solutions computationally

notebooks.title: Algorithms 01: Introductory Coding
objectives: Review basic control flow (loops, if/else) without using external libraries
Solve common algorithmic primitives from scratch
Learn basic testing methodologies
Develop strong debugging habits using print statements

notebooks.title: Algorithms 02: Number Systems
objectives: Understand base-2 (binary) and base-16 (hexadecimal) number systems
Convert numbers from base-b to decimal using positional coefficients
Convert decimal numbers to base-b using repeated division
Write modular conversion functions from scratch

notebooks.title: Algorithms 03: Recursive Sequences
objectives: Understand what a recursive sequence is
Compute sequence terms using iterative loops (arrays/variables)
Compute sequence terms using function recursion
Understand the time and space complexity tradeoffs between iteration and recursion

notebooks.title: Algorithms 04: Simulating Randomness
objectives: Understand Monte Carlo simulation
Simulate events using random number generators
Estimate complex probabilities computationally
Verify results using high-level sanity checks (distributions)

notebooks.title: Algorithms 05: Roulette Wheel Selection
objectives: Sample from discrete, non-uniform probability distributions
Compute a cumulative distribution function computationally
Apply Roulette Wheel Selection to pick an index

notebooks.title: Algorithms 06: Cartesian Product
objectives: Understand the Cartesian Product of multiple arrays
Understand the danger of array-referencing vs array-copying in Python
Implement an iterative Cartesian product generator from scratch

notebooks.title: Algorithms 07: Brute Force Cryptography
objectives: Understand encoding and decoding functions
Use brute-force search to explore a parameter space
Break a simple linear encryption scheme computationally

notebooks.title: Algorithms 08: Backtracking & Magic Squares
objectives: Understand how backtracking optimizes brute-force search
Use a `continue` statement to prune hopeless search branches
Solve the $3 \times 3$ Magic Square computationally

notebooks.title: Algorithms 09: Estimating Roots
objectives: Estimate roots of functions using Bisection Search
Estimate roots of functions using the Newton-Raphson Method
Compare the convergence rates of both algorithms

notebooks.title: Algorithms 10: Single Variable Gradient Descent
objectives: Understand the geometric intuition of Gradient Descent
Implement the gradient descent update rule computationally
Tune the learning rate $\alpha$ to prevent divergence
Handle Local Minima by testing multiple initial guesses

notebooks.title: Algorithms 11: Multivariable Gradient Descent
objectives: Understand gradients as vectors of partial derivatives
Apply the multivariable update rule computationally
Optimize functions of two or more variables

notebooks.title: Algorithms 12: Basic Sorting
objectives: Implement Selection Sort, Bubble Sort, and Insertion Sort from scratch
Implement Counting Sort and understand its memory tradeoffs
Analyze $O(n^2)$ time complexity

notebooks.title: Algorithms 13: Advanced Sorting
objectives: Understand "Divide and Conquer" algorithms
Implement Merge Sort recursively
Implement Quicksort recursively
Understand $O(n \log n)$ time complexity

notebooks.title: Algorithms 14: Basic Matrix Arithmetic
objectives: Design a `Matrix` class from scratch using 2D arrays
Implement matrix addition, subtraction, scalar multiplication, and transpose
Implement matrix multiplication using dot products
Compute matrix determinants recursively via cofactor expansion

notebooks.title: Algorithms 15: Reduced Row Echelon Form
objectives: Implement the RREF algorithm iteratively using helper functions
Compute the Inverse of a matrix much faster using augmented RREF
Compute Determinants in $O(N^3)$ time instead of $O(N!)$

notebooks.title: Algorithms 16: K-Means Clustering
objectives: Understand unsupervised learning and clustering
Implement the iterative K-Means algorithm
Use Euclidean distance to assign data points to clusters
Use the Elbow Method to determine the optimal number of clusters $K$

notebooks.title: Algorithms 17: Tic-Tac-Toe
objectives: Use Object-Oriented design to encapsulate game state
Enforce rules via private methods and move validation
Create Random and Manual Player classes that interact with the Game
Develop logging and debugging tools for game history

notebooks.title: Algorithms 18: Euler Estimation
objectives: Solve ordinary differential equations (ODEs) computationally using Euler's Method
Generalize 1D Euler Estimation to Multivariable Systems of ODEs
Use Matplotlib to visualize the trajectories of state variables

notebooks.title: Algorithms 19: SIR Model For the Spread of Disease
objectives: Formulate the SIR (Susceptible, Infected, Recovered) ODE equations
Understand population dynamics and transmission rates
Simulate an epidemic outbreak using Multivariable Euler Estimation

notebooks.title: Algorithms 20: Hodgkin-Huxley Model of Action Potentials
objectives: Translate massive systems of biological ODEs into Python code
Handle time-dependent stimulus functions $s(t)$
Simulate a neuron firing an Action Potential (Spike)

notebooks.title: Algorithms 21: Hash Tables
objectives: Understand how Dictionaries/Maps are implemented under the hood
Design a Hash Function
Implement a Hash Table with Buckets to handle collisions

notebooks.title: Algorithms 22: Simplex Method
objectives: Understand Linear Programming and Optimization under Constraints
Introduce Slack Variables to convert inequalities to equations
Implement the Simplex Tableau pivoting algorithm

notebooks.title: Algorithms 23-25: Regression via Pseudoinverse
objectives: Formulate curve-fitting as an overdetermined system of linear equations $y = Xp$
Use the Pseudoinverse $(X^T X)^{-1} X^T$ to find the optimal least-squares parameters $p$
Extend the pseudoinverse to fit Polynomial, Multiple Linear, Power, Exponential, and Logistic models via algebraic transformation

notebooks.title: Algorithms 26: Overfitting, Underfitting, and Bias-Variance Tradeoff
objectives: Understand why fitting a model to 100% accuracy on training data is dangerous
Diagnose High Bias (Underfitting) vs High Variance (Overfitting)
Implement Leave-One-Out Cross Validation to measure true generalization accuracy

notebooks.title: Algorithms 27-28: Regression via Gradient Descent & Interaction Terms
objectives: Formulate Logistic and Non-linear Regression without using the Pseudoinverse transform
Define a loss function (Residual Sum of Squares)
Compute partial derivatives to update weights using Gradient Descent
Use Interaction Terms to model complex multi-variable relationships

notebooks.title: Algorithms 29: K-Nearest Neighbors
objectives: Differentiate Regression from Classification
Implement the K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) classification algorithm
Use Euclidean Distance to measure point similarity
Use Leave-One-Out Cross Validation to choose the optimal hyperparameter $K$

notebooks.title: Algorithms 30: Naive Bayes Classification
objectives: Formulate classification using Bayes' Theorem
Understand the "Naive" independence assumption
Implement a probabilisitic classifier that calculates $P(Class | Data)$

notebooks.title: Algorithms 31: Breadth-First and Depth-First Search
objectives: Represent a Graph using Adjacency Lists and Adjacency Matrices
Implement Breadth-First Search (BFS) using a Queue
Implement Depth-First Search (DFS) using a Stack or Recursion
Differentiate the use-cases for BFS vs DFS

notebooks.title: Algorithms 32-33: Shortest Paths & Dijkstra's Algorithm
objectives: Define Weighted Graphs
Use a Priority Queue (Min-Heap)
Implement Dijkstra's Algorithm to find the shortest path from a start node to all other nodes
Track the actual path taken using a `previous` map

notebooks.title: Algorithms 34: Decision Trees
objectives: Formulate rules-based classification as a Tree structure
Compute Shannon Entropy to measure dataset impurity
Implement the recursive ID3 / CART algorithm to automatically grow a Decision Tree

notebooks.title: Algorithms 35-36: Artificial Neural Networks
objectives: Design a Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP)
Implement Forward Propagation using Matrix multiplications
Implement Backpropagation to calculate gradients across hidden layers
Train a neural network to solve the non-linear XOR problem

notebooks.title: Algorithms 37-39: Minimax & Game Trees
objectives: Formulate 2-player zero-sum games as a state-space Tree
Implement the Minimax Algorithm to guarantee optimal play
Integrate Minimax into your Tic-Tac-Toe architecture from Batch 3

notebooks.title: Algorithms 40-42: Neuroevolution & Blondie24
objectives: Combine Neural Networks and Evolutionary Algorithms
Replicate Fogel & Chellapilla's *Blondie24* checkers player
Train networks through pure self-play tournaments without datasets or backpropagation

notebooks.title: Algorithms 43: Convolutional Blondie24
objectives: Understand the spatial limits of standard MLPs
Design a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architecture
Re-implement the Convolutional layer of Blondie24
